title: Award-winning author Suzy Hansen says Turkish President Erdogan's grip on power not necessarily assured
gist: A podcast episode description previews author Suzy Hansen's new book on Turkey under President Erdogan, noting his core supporters increasingly feel betrayed and that his hold on power may not be permanent.

insights.claim: Suzy Hansen, who reported from Turkey for The New York Times Magazine for more than a decade, has written a new book examining how President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's core supporters are feeling betrayed by him.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: A credentialed long-term Turkey observer flags erosion of Erdogan's base as a live narrative — a potential leading indicator for Turkish political and market volatility if core support continues weakening.
insights.locator: Hansen, who spent more than a decade reporting

insights.claim: The episode description frames Erdogan's grip on power as 'not necessarily assured,' suggesting his multi-decade rule could end sooner than commonly assumed.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: A contrarian-leaning signal against consensus assumptions of Erdogan's durability — worth monitoring alongside Turkish political and currency risk.
insights.quote: grip on power not necessarily assured
insights.locator: Award-winning author Suzy Hansen says Turkish President Erdogan's
